title: AI-Driven Resume Quality & ATS Analysis System
description: Built an end-to-end AI hiring analysis system that ingested resumes, LinkedIn exports, GitHub metadata, and interview transcripts into a PostgreSQL knowledge graph, then scored candidate-to-job alignment using a custom ATS engine built on sentence-transformers and scikit-learn. Orchestrated a multi-agent LangGraph pipeline spanning OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama models to generate explainable skill gap analyses and targeted resume improvements, with validation logic that strictly grounded all outputs in existing candidate evidence to prevent hallucinated credentials. Delivered the complete system through an interactive Textual terminal UI, with reproducible ETL pipelines and documented prompt engineering practices enabling auditable, evidence-backed hiring decisions.

title: California Real Estate Price Prediction
description: Built a machine learning web application that predicts single-family residence prices across California using ensemble models including XGBoost and LightGBM, trained on housing market data with features such as location, square footage, and property characteristics. Developed an interactive Streamlit dashboard with address-based search functionality, allowing users to input property details and receive real-time price predictions alongside comparative market visualizations built with Plotly. The pipeline incorporated scikit-learn preprocessing, feature engineering, and model serialization via Joblib to deliver a deployable, end-to-end prediction system.

title: eCommerce Session Review Analysis - Predicting Online Shopper Purchase Intent
description: Built classification models using Random Forest and XGBoost on 12,330 e-commerce browsing sessions to predict purchase intent, applying threshold tuning to optimize recall and reduce missed conversions, achieving ~90% accuracy. Applied K-Means clustering to segment shoppers into five behavioral archetypes, uncovering conversion rates ranging from 0.4% for bounce-prone visitors to 24.3% for high-intent consumers. Identified product browsing depth and administrative page engagement as the strongest purchase predictors using Python, scikit-learn, pandas, and seaborn.

title: Dsc207miniproject
description: This project applied data wrangling, statistical analysis, and visualization techniques to a structured dataset using Python libraries including pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, and Seaborn. Data was retrieved via API requests and processed to uncover patterns and trends through exploratory data analysis and visual storytelling. The findings were presented in a Jupyter Notebook, demonstrating proficiency in the end-to-end data science workflow from ingestion to insight.

title: Sleepercelmanual
description: Built a manual, notebook-based implementation of a sleeper cell detection system using Python, NumPy, and Pandas to process and evaluate network or behavioral data through hand-crafted logic rather than automated pipelines. Leveraged the Requests library to fetch external datasets and applied custom filtering and aggregation techniques to identify dormant or low-activity patterns indicative of sleeper cell behavior. The project served as a ground-up reference implementation to validate methodology before scaling to a fully automated version.

title: SleeperCel
description: Built a full-stack AI roster building assistant for fantasy football that integrates with the public Sleeper API to display user leagues, rosters, and available players in real time. The frontend was developed using React and Vite, while the backend leveraged Python with pandas and NumPy to process player data and support roster optimization logic. The project combined live API data retrieval with data-driven player evaluation to help users make informed lineup decisions.

title: Bike Traffic in Cambridge Interactive Visualization
description: Built an interactive visualization of bicycle traffic patterns across Cambridge using JavaScript, CSS, and HTML. Mapped bike lane usage and traffic volume data onto a dynamic interface, allowing users to filter and inspect ridership trends across different streets and neighborhoods. The project demonstrated how dedicated bike infrastructure correlates with higher cycling activity throughout the city.

title: Desktop Tutorial
description: This tutorial project walked through the foundational GitHub Desktop workflow, demonstrating how to initialize a repository, edit files locally, and commit changes through a graphical interface rather than the command line. The project used a simple README modification as a hands-on exercise to practice staging and committing tracked file changes. The result was a functional local-to-remote repository pipeline, establishing a repeatable version control process for future projects.

title: How Airbnb Tourism was impacted by the San Diego Sunshine
description: A static graph which explores Airbnb bookings and revenue throughout the year in San Diego, California. The graph shows how the number of bookings and revenue changes throughout the year, with the highest peaks in the summer months.

title: Study on the Impact of Estrus on Female Rats Throughout the Day
description: This graph is a static visualization of the body temperature across male and female mice. Used to explore differences in the daily cylce of body temperature while highlighting the impact of female estrus.

title: AllRecipes Rating Predictive Classification Model
description: This machine learning model predicts the ratings of recipes on AllRecipes.com based on features of the recipe. We created a multiclass-classifier using a RandomForest.

title: Study of the Presence of Educational Institutions and Income Throughout California
description: An exploratory data analysis of the presence of educational institutions and income throughout California. The project uses a heatmap to show the distribution of educational institutions and income throughout the state of California.

title: Image Processor
description: Built an image processor that can read in an image, apply a filter to it, and then output the processed image. The image processor can apply filters such as negate, grayscale, and edge detection. This project was built using Python.

title: Asteroid Landfall Simulation
description: Written using pandas in the Jupyter Notebook environment, this project simulates the landfall of asteroids using historical datasets, probabilistic modeling, and bootstrapping.

title: Interactive Visualization of Mice Body Temperature Across Gender
description: An interactive visualization of the body temperature across the gender of mice. The visualization allows users to explore the differences in body temperature while allowing filtering and the altering of scope. Features a tooltip which highlights Mouse ID allowing us to examine indvidual mice along with their estrus cycle( should they be female).
